prompt:
Using the attached photograph, produce the SAME EXACT SCENE but with the desk lamp SWITCHED OFF. The room is now in near-darkness.

KEEP, EXACTLY AS THEY ARE, IN THE SAME POSITIONS:
- the three vertical columns of small pinned printed tickets on the deep navy plaster wall (INTAKE / TRIAGE / AGENT / REVIEW / OUTPUT with timestamps)
- the brass thumbtacks pinning each ticket
- the physical desk lamp itself, still positioned at camera-right (the bulb is off now, but the lamp body remains exactly where it was)
- the low stool with the ceramic mug of cold coffee and the closed leather notebook
- the deep navy plaster wall, the wood floor or surface, all physical objects
- the camera angle, focal length, depth of field, film grain, aspect ratio

CHANGE:
- the desk lamp is OFF - no warm pool of light on the lower wall, no warm tungsten glow, no rim light catching the cards
- the scene is now lit only by VERY DIM ambient blue moonlight from off-frame, just enough to barely make out the silhouettes of the cards on the wall and the stool below
- the warm tones become deep cool navy darkness everywhere
- the cards on the wall are barely visible, suggested rather than read - mostly dark with the faintest hint of paper texture
- the lamp shade has a tiny cool moonlight rim on its top edge only
- the overall image reads as 'the operator turned off the light and left' - quiet, finished, dark

FORBID: any text overlay, any web UI, any glow, any persimmon orange, any added object, any human or person, any pure pitch-black (it is dim, not pitch-black - the cards must still be barely perceptible silhouettes), any color shift away from deep navy/cool darkness, any change to object positions.
